checks.a_analysis: The output accurately represents the text found in the source document without inventing new content. The headers, meeting details, agenda items, and meeting notes match the source. AI-generated elements like the metadata title and image alt text are within acceptable parameters.
The phone number (984-204-1487) and Conference ID (350 773 333#) found on Page 1 were checked against the JSON output. The information matches perfectly.

The document conversion covers both pages of the source PDF. All primary agenda items (1 through 15) and the footer notes are represented in the output. While significant internal content is missing (see C5), the sections themselves are not omitted.

The visual agenda table in the source spans two pages. The JSON converts the first page part (items 1-4) into a table and subsequent items (5-15) into lists. While the table element itself has fewer rows, the information from the missing rows (the main agenda items and presenters) is preserved in the list structure. The loss of sub-item data is addressed in Check C5.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: There is a massive omission of sub-content throughout the document. For Item 4 (Consent Agenda), all financial data for the NG 911 Reserve Fund, CMRS Account, PSAP Account, and Grant/Statewide Projects Account (June/July balances and disbursements) is missing. For Item 5 (Executive Director's Report), sub-items a) through f) are missing. For Item 9 (Grant Committee Report), all sub-items (a-c) and the associated bullet points are missing. For Item 13 (Education Committee Report), all sub-items (a-d) are missing. This constitutes a loss of more than 50% of the document's total text content.
checks.b_evidence_source: Item 4b-e contains financial figures (e.g., '$ 76,125,411', '$ 2,419,955'). Item 5 contains sub-items a-f (e.g., 'a) Biennial Audit Status', 'c) NextGen 911 GIS Status'). Item 9 contains sub-items and bullets (e.g., '• Onslow County 911'). Item 13 contains sub-items a-d (e.g., 'a) Training Course Request...').
checks.b_evidence_output: The output only lists the headers for these items. For example, reading_order 5 just lists headers like 'b) NG 911 Reserve Fund –' without the data. reading_order 6 and 9 similarly omit the sub-lists for items 5, 9, and 13.
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections 4, 5, 9, and 13 across Page 1 and Page 2.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text that was captured is largely accurate. Minor changes like adding asterisks for emphasis in item 4 are acceptable reformatting.

checks.a_analysis: The output JSON represents the content of the agenda without any detectable duplication of sections, paragraphs, or lists. Each agenda item and header appears once, mirroring the source document structure.
The heading hierarchy correctly uses H1 for the main title 'AGENDA' and H2 for the sub-title 'NORTH CAROLINA 911 BOARD MEETING'. No subsequent heading levels skip levels or are used incorrectly.

The table structure is corrupted. The header row defines 3 columns (Tab, Topic, Presenter). However, the subsequent data rows do not maintain this structure. Some rows contain only 1 cell (e.g., 'Call to Order'), while others contain 2 cells (e.g., Tab index '1.' in one cell and the topic/presenter text in the second). The content for 'Topic' and 'Presenter' is merged into a single cell, failing to represent the table's logical organization as seen in the source.
checks.b_evidence_source:
Tab | Topic | Presenter
... | Call to Order | Jim Weaver
1. | Chair's Opening Remarks | Jim Weaver

checks.a_analysis: The overall reading order follows the flow of the document from top to bottom, from the header through the agenda items and meeting closing. While some items are missing (covered under S5), the flow of included content matches the source.

The list conversion is failing to capture multiple sub-items and nested list structures. Specifically: 1) The sub-items a) through f) under item '5. Executive Director’s Report' are completely missing. 2) The bulleted sub-items under item '9. Grant Committee Report' are missing. 3) The sub-items a) through d) under item '13. Education Committee Report' are missing. This results in a loss of the source's structural detail.
checks.b_evidence_source:
5. Executive Director’s Report... Pokey Harris
 a) Biennial Audit Status
 b) NextGen 911 Migration Status...
 9. Grant Committee Report...
 • Onslow County 911...
 13. Education Committee Report...
 a) Training Course Request...
checks.b_evidence_output: The output list items for 5, 9, and 13 only contain the main line text and omit all sub-items.

checks.a_analysis: There are no links in the output document. While the source mentions a link for the meeting packet, no clickable URL is visible in the text to be converted, and no fabricated links were added.

checks.a_analysis: The source PDF maintains a consistent three-column layout (Tab, Topic, Presenter) for all agenda items throughout both pages. The HTML output breaks this consistency: it renders the initial items (1-4) in a formal table structure, but then switches to a single-column bulleted list for items 5 through 15. This significant change in visual organization disrupts the intended flow and structure of the document.
checks.b_evidence_source: The PDF shows columns for 'Tab', 'Topic', and 'Presenter' for all items from 'Call to Order' through item 15.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML rendering displays a table for items 1-4, followed by a bulleted list for item 5 ('Executive Director's Report') through item 15.
checks.b_evidence_location: Main agenda list, spanning pages 1 and 2 of the source PDF.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML rendering fails to visually represent the tabular financial data found in sections 4b, 4c, 4d, and 4e of the source PDF. In the PDF, these sections contain clear columns of labels (e.g., 'June 2022 Fund balance') and corresponding dollar amounts. In the HTML, this entire structured data block is missing, with only the section headings remaining. Additionally, the header for the main agenda table is visually truncated from 'Presenter' to 'Present'.
checks.b_evidence_source: Financial data rows under sections 4b-4e, such as 'June 2022 Fund balance $ 76,125,411'. Also, the table header 'Presenter'.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML only renders the sub-section headers (e.g., 'b) NG 911 Reserve Fund –') without the data rows. The main table header is truncated to 'Present'.
checks.b_evidence_location: Section 4, sub-items b, c, d, e; Table header at the top of the agenda.

checks.a_analysis: The meaningful logos (NC DIT logo) from the source PDF are present in the HTML output. While their size and positioning differ, the graphical content is preserved.
No instances of duplicated body content (paragraphs, list items, or tables) were observed in the HTML output.
